texto: I knew in that moment, we were never meant to surrender our childlike innocence, to trade a world in which we fit like a glove for one that hung on us like ill-fitting hand-me-downs. However, all about us insisted on our membership. And instead of a handshake or a mystical password as entrance into this spurious society, we agreed instead to share a lie, the one that says we’re safe, secure, and fulfilled living this way.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Christina Carson: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “I knew in that moment, we were never meant to surrender our childlike innocence, to trade a world…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que amizade, cancer, health-and-healing pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de termino e amizade.

texto: But here in my hometown, history was like a fine dust that settled out on everything. There was nothing to counter it. The culture had been hardened by a religion suspect of joy, yet fascinated by sin. Its moral acceptance of slavery eroded compassion. And gentility became a necessary pretense to cover the resentment created long ago when the North’s industrial prestige trumped the agrarian South. It was not an easy place to feel lighthearted or triumphant. Nor was it an easy place to remember the beauty of wonder and awe.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Christina Carson: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. O trecho “But here in my hometown, history was like a fine dust that settled out on everything. texto: It struck me that the beauty we attribute to children isn’t something they have that we don’t. It's something they do, which we have long since stopped doing—just describing things as we see them, the simple, unadorned facts.
